title: Thar'quan

{{Quote|Over the years, Thar'quan has disabled many an Imperial facility. ''[…]'' As a Defel, he is a natural infiltration and sabotage agent. Despite the horrible injuries he has suffered, he continues to serve the Alliance's cause.|General Airen Cracken|<ref name="CRO" />}}
'''Thar'quan''' was a combat-trained [[Defel/Legends|Defel]] [[Miner/Legends|miner]] and a [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteur]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Working as an [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|intelligence agent]] for [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]], Thar'quan carried out attacks on assets of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], including the [[capture of Alpha Outpost]]. He performed other dangerous missions while working undercover. Thar'quan was painfully scarred several times in the line of duty, but that did not affect his enthusiasm, loyalty or efficiency.

==Biography==
A [[Defel/Legends|Defel]] born on [[Af'El/Legends|Af'El]] in [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]], Thar'quan eventually became a [[Miner/Legends|miner]] and a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. He was a [[Spy/Legends|spy]], [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteur]] and information collector for Rebel [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]. Under the guise of a rough miner equipped with dark gray and black gear common to Defel miners, Thar'quan hacked [[Computer/Legends|computer]] networks at [[Uhur'qah]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of Af'El, to benefit the Rebel network on the nearby [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sriluur/Legends|Sriluur]].<ref name="CRO" />

During his career, Thar'quan had an unfortunate encounter with a [[Dithanune]] [[needle beast]] that managed to injure him. He was also injured during an unplanned trek through a [[razor grass]] field on [[Togoria/Legends|Togoria]]. At some other point, Thar'quan served a sentence in a [[Kessel prison]] and suffered an accident affecting his face while he was there.<ref name="CRO" /> Thar'quan was eventually rescued from Kessel by Rebel [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Larq Thur]].<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref>

For some time, Thar'quan was an employee for [[Hctaqsas Metals]] while working undercover for the Alliance along with Rebel agent [[Sasnak Toxis]]. Thar'quan was wounded due to the dangers of this activity.<ref name="CRO" />

At some point, Thar'quan managed to [[Capture of Alpha Outpost|capture]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Alpha Outpost]] during an operation to distract the Empire's attention from Rebel activities on [[Ralltiir/Legends|Ralltiir]]. A short time after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Thar'quan managed to access the top-secret coordinates of [[Ore/Legends|ore]] depots of [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]]. Soon after this operation, Thar'quan went to Uhur'qah.<ref name="CRO" />

==Personality and traits==
A hard-working and loyal member of the Rebel Alliance, Thar'quan was an expert at blind-fighting and with the [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-pickaxe]], although he commonly used a [[Blaster carbine/Legends|blaster carbine]]; a [[Garrote/Legends|garrote]]; and a sawed-off [[Adostic Arms/Legends|Adostic Arms]] 8-gauge projectile [[Shotgun/Legends|shotgun]]. He was also familiar with the bureaucracy of the mining companies he had worked for, particularly [[Vulca Minerals]] and Hctaqsas Metals.<ref name="CRO" />

Thar'quan had a collection of scars from his activities as a Rebel scout, although beings unaware of his real activities believed Thar'quan had suffered mining accidents. These scars included ragged punctured holes on his right ear after his encounter with a Dithanune needle beast, partial paralysis of his right cheek due to an accident in a Kessel prison, disfigurement of his back and side suffered in a razor grass field on Togoria, and other scars suffered while working for Hctaqsas Metals along with Toxis. He proudly showed these cicatrices to Rebel collaborators—Cracken himself admired Thar'quan due to his loyalty toward the Alliance despite his suffering.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Thar'quan was first mentioned in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', written by several authors and edited by [[Bill Smith]]. Thar'quan was alluded to again, this time in passing, in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''. This last book misspells his name as ''Tar'quan'' and ''Tar'quin''.
